Japanese: 同日　午後　３時３６分 地方裁判所　第７法廷
English: February 10, 3:36 PM District Court Courtroom No. 7

Japanese: 審理に入る前に‥‥
English: Now then, before we proceed any further...

Japanese: 先ほど、検査の結果が届きました ので、ご報告しましょう。
English: ...I'm going to announce the results of the tests we had performed earlier.
Tests...?

Japanese: この小刀の血痕です。 現場の、松の木に刺さっていた‥‥
English: Yes, tests. On the bloody dagger that was found stuck in the pine tree.

Japanese: あ、ああ！　アレ、ですか。 （‥‥すっかり、忘れてた）
English: Oh yeah! That! (I totally forgot about it.)

Japanese: 綾里　真宵が‥‥、被害者に “反撃”した際の《凶器》‥‥だ。
English: That's the weapon that Maya Fey used when she fought with the victim.

Japanese: それで‥‥結果は！ 血痕は、やっぱり、被害者の‥‥？
English: So what are the results!? Was it the victim's blood, or...?

Japanese: 時間の関係で、精密な検査は できませんでしたが‥‥
English: Due to time constraints, a full test wasn't possible. However...

Japanese: １つだけ、ハッキリ 断言できることがあります。
English: There's one thing we can say with certainty.

Japanese: ‥‥この小刀に付着した血液は、 被害者のものではありません！
English: The blood that was on this dagger was not the victim's blood!

Japanese: ‥‥以上です。 それでは、審理を再開しましょう。
English: ...That is all. Now then, let's restart this trial.

Japanese: （小刀に付着している血は、 舞子さんのものではない‥‥）
English: (So it wasn't Misty's blood on the dagger...)

Japanese: （じゃあ、いったいダレの‥‥？）
English: (Then whose was it...?)

Japanese: 証拠品《小刀》のデータを 書きなおした。
English: Dagger updated in the Court Record.

Japanese: 検察側・弁護側とも おわかりでしょうが‥‥
English: I'm sure both the defense and the prosecution know this, but...

Japanese: 本件の審理は、 これが最後になります。
English: ...this trial is rapidly coming to a close.

Japanese: 両者には、決定的な立証を 要請します。
English: Both sides will need to show some firm evidence with their claims.

Japanese: わかりました。
English: I understand, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥聞くところによると‥‥
English: From what I've heard...

Japanese: 証人の体力は、限界に近いらしい。 ‥‥一気に行くぜ。
English: ...the witness is dangerously weak, physically speaking. So, let's finish this quickly.

Japanese: ‥‥モチロンです。
English: ...Agreed.

Japanese: それでは！　最後の証人を 入廷させてください！
English: Very well! Please bring in the last witness!

Japanese: ‥‥証人。名前と職業を 聞かせてもらえるかい。
English: ...Witness, please tell us your name and profession.

Japanese: 綾里　真宵です。 職業は‥‥職業は‥‥‥
English: Maya Fey. My profession is... Umm...

Japanese: 成歩堂法律事務所の 副所長、です。
English: I'm the assistant manager at Wright & Co. Law Offices.

Japanese: （‥‥真宵ちゃん‥‥）
English: (Maya...)

Japanese: ここにある資料によれば、 あなたは‥‥
English: According to the magazine I have here...

Japanese: 倉院流霊媒道の霊媒師、 ということですが‥‥
English: ...you're a spirit medium of the Kurain Channeling Technique...

Japanese: あたし‥‥恐いんです。
English: I... I'm frightened.

Japanese: 綾里家の‥‥ 血を引いていることが。
English: The Fey clan... I don't want any more to do with it.

Japanese: （綾里の“血”をめぐる事件‥‥ ムリもない、か‥‥）
English: (Oh, Maya... The pain the Fey bloodline causes must be unbearable...)

Japanese: ‥‥よろしい。
English: ...Very well.

Japanese: さて、綾里　真宵さん。
English: Now then, Ms. Fey.

Japanese: あなたは、事件が起こったとき‥‥ 《奥の院》の中庭にいました。
English: When the event occurred... you were in the garden of the Inner Temple.

Japanese: そして‥‥‥
English: And...

Japanese: 天流斎　エリスさんが殺害された 瞬間を、目撃した。
English: You witnessed the moment of Ms. Elise Deauxnim's murder.

Japanese: ‥‥まちがいありませんか？
English: ...Is this correct?

Japanese: あ、あ。あの、あたし‥‥ なんにも見ていませ
English: I, umm... I... I didn't see anyth--

Japanese: カオを上げるんだ、 おじょうちゃん。
English: Straighten up this moment, young lady!
Huh...?

Japanese: 目をそらさずに、今は前を見ろ。 ‥‥泣くのは、いつでもできる。
English: Pick your head up and speak clearly. There's always time for crying later.

Japanese: で‥‥でも！
English: B-But I...

Japanese: アンタの母親は、 アンタの目の前で殺害された！
English: Your mother was killed right in front of your eyes!

Japanese: そのジジツは、もう‥‥ 変えられねえのさ。
English: There's nothing you can do to change that fact.

Japanese: アンタにできることは、ひとつ。 ‥‥決着をつけることだ。
English: But there's something you can do... You can finish this.

Japanese: 今まで‥‥さんざん、 見てきただろう？
English: You've been watching the whole thing right?

Japanese: 証人たちが、身をけずり‥‥ コトバをしぼり出す、その姿を。
English: You've seen the witnesses come out and you've seen us squeeze the truth out of them.

Japanese: アンタの番だ。 ‥‥証言してもらうぜ。
English: Now it's your turn. ...Let's hear your testimony.

Japanese: 事件当夜‥‥アンタの目の前で、 何が起こったのか！
English: On the night of the crime... what exactly did you see happen!?

Japanese: ‥‥証人。おねがいします。
English: Witness. If you please.

Japanese: はい。わかりました。
English: ...Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: ～《奥の院》で起こったこと～
English: -- At the Inner Temple --

Japanese: はなれの間で修行の用意をして、 中庭を通りかかったときでした。
English: I was passing through the garden on the way to a spare prep room when it happened.

Japanese: いきなり、あの。だれかにアタマを ポカッて殴られたんです‥‥。
English: Suddenly, someone struck me over the head...

Japanese: あたし、よろめいてしまって‥‥ 灯ろうにもたれかかりました。
English: I stumbled... and ended up against the stone lantern.

Japanese: 思わず『助けてください！』って ‥‥叫んだような気がします。
English: I think I screamed, "Help me!"

Japanese: 次の瞬間‥‥あたしの身体に、何か あたたかいものがふりかかって‥‥
English: Then something warm splashed over me...

Japanese: あたし‥‥そのまま、 気を失っちゃいました。
English: That's when... I lost consciousness.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ “ポカッ”と‥‥
English: Hmm... So you were struck on the head...

Japanese: もちろん、このツエ だったのでしょうな。
English: I suppose it must have been this staff.

Japanese: それで、真宵ちゃん！ きみを殴ったのは‥‥
English: Maya! The person who hit you...

Japanese: やっぱり、美柳　ちなみ だったんだね！
English: It was Dahlia Hawthorne, wasn't it!?

Japanese: ご‥‥ごめんね、なるほどくん。
English: I-I'm sorry, Nick.

Japanese: あたし‥‥わからないの。 よく、見えなかったから‥‥
English: I just... I couldn't see... I don't know who it was...

Japanese: ‥‥そう、なのか‥‥
English: Maya, think hard...

Japanese: あ。ほ、ホントだからね！
English: Sorry, Nick, but I really couldn't!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
若い女の子には、おすすめできない 夜だったらしいぜ。
English: Can't say it was an especially good night for young ladies to be walking around alone.

Japanese: どうやら、証言から犯人を特定 するのは、ムズカシイようですな。
English: It seems that it will be hard to determine the criminal through testimony alone.

Japanese: それでは、弁護人。 ‥‥尋問をおねがいしましょう。
English: Very well then, Mr. Wright. Please begin your cross-examination...

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん‥‥）
English: (Maya... Hang in there...)

Japanese: （どうも‥‥ ようすがオカシイな‥‥）
English: (She doesn't look well at all...)

Japanese: ～《奥の院》で起こったこと～
English: -- At the Inner Temple --

Japanese: はなれの間で修行の用意をして、 中庭を通りかかったときでした。
English: I was passing through the garden on the way to a spare prep room when it happened.

Japanese: いきなり、あの。だれかにアタマを ポカッて殴られたんです‥‥。
English: Suddenly, someone struck me over the head...

Japanese: あたし、よろめいてしまって‥‥ 灯ろうにもたれかかりました。
English: I stumbled... and ended up against the stone lantern.

Japanese: 思わず『助けてください！』って ‥‥叫んだような気がします。
English: I think I screamed, "Help me!"

Japanese: 次の瞬間‥‥あたしの身体に、何か あたたかいものがふりかかって‥‥
English: Then something warm splashed over me...

Japanese: あたし‥‥そのまま、 気を失っちゃいました。
English: That's when... I lost consciousness.

Japanese: （‥‥真宵ちゃんとは、 もう３年もいっしょにいる）
English: (I've worked side by side with Maya for 3 years now.)

Japanese: （何かをかくしていれば‥‥ ぼくには、タイドでわかる）
English: (When she hides something, I can tell by how she acts.)

Japanese: （そして、今‥‥）
English: (And right now...)

Japanese: （彼女は、まちがいなく 何かをかくしている！）
English: (I'm absolutely certain she's hiding something!)

Japanese: 灯ろうのあかりで見えたんです。 ワルモノの後ろに、男の人が‥‥
English: I could see a man behind my attacker by the light of the stone lantern.

Japanese: あの晩‥‥《奥の院》側には、 だれがいたの？
English: Who else was on the Inner Temple side that night?

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥も、モチロン、 毘忌尼さんだけだよ！
English: Well, Sister Bikini of course!

Japanese: “あとで、あやめさんも来るから” ‥‥って、言ってたけど。
English: But I remember her saying that Iris would be coming later as well.

Japanese: （ビキニさんは、事件の翌日 葉桜院で、御剣に会っている）
English: (On the day after the incident, Edgeworth met Bikini at Hazakura Temple.)

Japanese: （したがって‥‥ 《犯人ではあり得ない》か）
English: (That means she couldn't be the criminal.)

Japanese: それで‥‥真宵ちゃんは、 あやめさんには会ったの？
English: So then... did Iris actually come later like she said?

Japanese: え‥‥ええと‥‥ どうだったっけなあ‥‥
English: Well, umm... Let me think...

Japanese: （‥‥コンランしてるみたいだ。 つかれているせいか‥‥）
English: (She seems confused. It's probably because she's so tired...)

Japanese: ううん‥‥。 たしか、会ってないなあ。
English: No... I'm sure I didn't see her.

Japanese: たぶん、あたしが修行の用意を してるとき、来たんだと思います。
English: She must have come while I was preparing for the training.

Japanese: いったい‥‥ダレに！
English: Who did it? Who hit you?

Japanese: あ‥‥あの。それが‥‥
English: Ah... Well...

Japanese: ‥‥見てないの。 殴られたの、後ろからだったし。
English: I didn't see who it was... I was hit from behind.

Japanese: み、“見てない”‥‥？
English: You didn't see the person...?

Japanese: でも！　その後、襲撃者は 目の前に立ちふさがったはずだ！
English: But after that, your attacker was in front of you!

Japanese: “見てない”はずが ないじゃないか！
English: How could you not see who it was!?

Japanese: ‥‥うッ！ ‥‥そ。そう言われてみれば‥‥
English: Oh! L-Let me think for a sec...

Japanese: ごめんね。なんだか‥‥ よく、思い出せなくて。
English: I'm sorry... I just can't remember.

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん、ツラそうだな。 ‥‥どうしよう）
English: (Maya is really having a hard time... What should I do?)

Japanese: （‥‥現場の状況を 冷静に考えると‥‥）
English: (Considering the conditions at the crime scene...)

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんが襲撃者の姿を 見ていないのも、ムリはない）
English: (...it's not surprising she didn't see her attacker.)

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
アンタにも、“情け”は あるらしいな‥‥
English: So it looks like even you've got some compassion.

Japanese: 今までの証言から考えると、 その“襲撃者”の正体は‥‥
English: Judging by the testimony thus far, the identity of the attacker was most likely...

Japanese: 天流斎　エリスさんが霊媒した、 美柳　ちなみ‥‥です。
English: ...The spirit that was channeled by Elise Deauxnim... Dahlia Hawthorne!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、証言を つづけていただきましょう。
English: Very well, please continue with your testimony.

Japanese: アタマを殴られてから、 どうなったか‥‥？
English: What happened after you were struck on the head...?

Japanese: ‥‥聞かせてもらえるかな、 真宵ちゃん。
English: ...Come on, Maya, this isn't making sense.

Japanese: なぜ、襲撃者の姿を “見ていない”のか‥‥？
English: Why didn't you see this person?

Japanese: ‥‥ええと、なんでだっけ。 あれは‥‥そのお‥‥。
English: Umm, well, let me think... It was... Uh...

Japanese: あ！　そうだ！ “暗かったから”だよ！
English: Oh yeah! That's right! It was dark, that's it!

Japanese: “暗かった”‥‥？
English: It was dark...?

Japanese: 霊行道の修行では、 よけいな光をきらうんです。
English: It's not good to have too much light around when someone is undergoing spiritual training.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥そういえば、 そういう証言がありましたな。
English: Hmm... Come to think of it, there was earlier testimony to that effect as well.

Japanese: ‥‥事件当時‥‥ 《中庭は、暗かった》。
English: So it was dark in the garden on the night of the crime...

Japanese: （だから、襲撃者の姿が “見えなかった”わけか‥‥）
English: (Which is why she didn't see her attacker...)

Japanese: （‥‥何か、ここに《秘密》が あると思ったのにな‥‥）
English: (I thought maybe she was trying to hide something, but I guess not...)

Japanese: そして‥‥どうなったんだ！
English: What happened afterwards!?

Japanese: あたしの前に、ワルモノが 立ちふさがった‥‥んだと思うの。
English: My attacker was in front of me, blocking off my escape. ...I think.
Y-You... think...?

Japanese: 思いっきり殴られて、あたし‥‥ もう、パニックになっちゃって。
English: After getting hit like that... I-I was in a state of panic.

Japanese: アタマの中を、おんなじコトバが ぐるぐる回ってたんです。
English: There were only two things rolling around in my mind at the time.

Japanese: ほほほお‥‥！ それは、キョーミ深いですな。
English: Well now...! This is beginning to get interesting!

Japanese: ちなみに、どんなコトバが ぐるぐる回っていたのかな？
English: So what were the two things rolling around in your mind?
Umm...

Japanese: 《あたしの名前は、綾里　真宵。 １たす１は、２》‥‥って。
English: "My name is Maya Fey. One plus one equals two"...

Japanese: あたし、記憶をなくさなかったか どうかが、シンパイだったんです。
English: You see, for some crazy reason, I was afraid I was going to lose my memory.

Japanese: ‥‥ホント、おかしいですよね。 イノチを狙われてたのに‥‥
English: I know it's odd considering my life was in danger, but that's how it was...

Japanese: ‥‥本当に、 危ないところでしたね。
English: That's not odd at all. Your actions are understandable, given the circumstances.

Japanese: それで‥‥あなたは、 どうしたのですかな？
English: So... What did you do then?

Japanese: “叫んだような気がする”‥‥ ハッキリしないの？
English: You think you screamed, but you're not sure?

Japanese: あたし‥‥もう、 ムチュウだったからね！
English: Listen, I was a complete wreck!

Japanese: ワルモノの姿も、 暗くてゼンゼン見えなくて‥‥
English: It was dark and I couldn't see my attacker...

Japanese: 男のヒトか、女のヒトか？ オトナか、コドモか？
English: Was it a man? A woman? An adult? A child?

Japanese: ‥‥なんにも、わからないの。 本当に、コワかったんだよ！
English: I had no idea... I was scared out of my wits!

Japanese: たしかに‥‥。この私ですら、 少しひるんだかもしれませんな。
English: Believe me, my dear... I'm certain I would have soiled my robes.

Japanese: そいつが、いつ襲ってくるかも わからなかった。だから‥‥
English: I thought this person might attack me, so I... so I...

Japanese: とにかく、何か叫んだの。 ‥‥それが最後の希望だと思って。
English: Anyway, I'm pretty sure I screamed... I thought that it was my last hope.

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん‥‥かなり、 コンランしているみたいだな）
English: (Wow, it sounds like poor little Maya really was out of her mind.)

Japanese: （言ってるコトが、よく わからなかったぞ‥‥）
English: (But I wonder what she meant by "last hope"...)

Japanese: （どうする？　詳しく 聞いてみるか‥‥？）
English: (What do I do? Do I press her for more details...?)

Japanese: （‥‥真宵ちゃんは今、 身もココロもつかれきっている）
English: (Maya is really emotionally and physically exhausted.)

Japanese: （あまり、問いつめたくない。 とりあえず、ようすを見よう）
English: (I shouldn't go overboard. For the time being, I'll just play it by ear.)

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、証人。 証言をつづけてください。
English: ...Alright then, witness. Go on with your testimony.

Japanese: え。あ‥‥はい。
English: Oh. Um... OK.

Japanese: 《助けてください》って 叫んだのは、まちがいないのかな？
English: So you're certain that you screamed, "Help me!"?

Japanese: ど‥‥どうかなあ。
English: Umm... I think so.

Japanese: よく、おぼえてないけど‥‥ たぶん、そうだと思います。
English: Now that I think about it... Yeah, that's gotta be what I screamed.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
《助けてくれ》と叫んだのが、 そんなにヘンなことかい？
English: Is it all that strange, Trite, for her to have screamed, "Help me"?

Japanese: （でも‥‥ なんとなく、気になる‥‥）
English: (No, but... Something about that bugs me...)

Japanese: （自分のイノチを狙っている 人間に向かって‥‥）
English: (Why would you face someone that's trying to kill you...)

Japanese: （わざわざ《助けてください》 なんて、叫ぶだろうか？）
English: (...and go through the trouble of screaming for help?)

Japanese: ちょっと待って、真宵ちゃん。 ‥‥“最後の希望”というのは？
English: Wait a minute, Maya. What's this "my last hope" stuff?

Japanese: え‥‥え！　なに、なに？ “最後の希望”‥‥って？
English: Um, what...? What do you mean by "your last hope"?

Japanese: いやいや！ 真宵ちゃんが言ったんだよ。
English: No, no, no! That's what you said...! You said, "my last hope."

Japanese: あ、あたし‥‥ そんなコト、言ったっけ。
English: Huh? What? I said what?

Japanese: きみは、姿の見えない襲撃者の方へ 向かって、叫んだんだよね？
English: Look, you were facing an attacker that you couldn't see and you screamed, right?

Japanese: 《助けてください！》って。
English: You screamed, "Help me!"

Japanese: う‥‥うん。
English: Um, yeah.

Japanese: それが“最後の希望だった” ‥‥きみは、そう証言しているよ。
English: But you testified that you screamed that because you thought it was your last hope.

Japanese: あ‥‥ああ。そのことか。 ええと‥‥なんだっけなあ‥‥
English: Oh... Well, you know... That's like... What do you call it when that happens...

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん‥‥大丈夫なのかな。 こんな状態で‥‥）
English: (Maya's not doing so well up there...)

Japanese: あのね。ええと‥‥ あ。そうだ！
English: Oh, yeah. Umm, I... Oh! That's right!

Japanese: あたし‥‥ワルモノに向かって 叫んだわけじゃないんだよ。
English: I remember now... I was facing my attacker, but that's not who I was screaming at.

Japanese: な、なんだって‥‥？
English: Wh-What did you just say...?

Japanese: たしか、あのとき‥‥
English: Yeah, that's right...

Japanese: ワルモノの後ろに、 あの人が立っていたの。
English: It was the person behind my attacker that I was yelling at.

Japanese: だから、あたし。そのヒトに 『助けてください！』って‥‥
English: That's who I was screaming to for help!

Japanese: ‥‥あああああああっ！
English: ...Aaaaaaaaaaaaah!

Japanese: こ、今度はどうしたの！
English: Wh-What is it now!?

Japanese: しまった！　これ、 だまっておこうと思ったのに‥‥
English: I messed up! I didn't... I didn't mean to let that slip out...
Huh...?

Japanese: しょ、証人！ 今の証言は‥‥本当ですかッ！
English: W-Witness! Are you absolutely sure of what you're saying?

Japanese: 襲撃者の後ろに‥‥ もうヒトリの人物がいた！
English: Behind the attacker... There was another person!?

Japanese: あ、いえ‥‥あたし。それは、 かくしておくつもりで‥‥あ。
English: Um, I am... Well... I uhh, I meant to keep that part a secret.

Japanese: うううう‥‥。 ナニやってんだろ、あたし‥‥
English: Nngh... What have I done?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
‥‥ホンネを聞きたかったら、 極限状態をねらえ。
English: "It takes a ton of pressure to make a diamond."

Japanese: それが、オレのルールだぜ。
English: That's what I always say.

Japanese: きょくげんじょうたい‥‥
English: A ton of pressure...

Japanese: ‥‥今のアンタは、 追いつめられている。
English: ...You're in a court of law here!

Japanese: ウソを考えたり、かくしごとをする ヨユウなんか、ねえのさ。
English: You can't make things up or try to hide things in this chamber.

Japanese: ‥‥証人。今の発言は、 言うまでもなく重要です。
English: ...Witness. The information you just presented is vital to the case.

Japanese: 証言に加えていただきましょう！
English: I want you to add it to your testimony!

Japanese: も、もしかして、それは‥‥？
English: Y-You don't mean...! W-Was it...?

Japanese: そのときは、よく わからなかったけど‥‥
English: I didn't know it at the time, but...

Japanese: たぶん‥‥血、だったと 思います。
English: I... I think it was probably blood.

Japanese: （《襲撃者》‥‥エリスさんが 霊媒した、美柳　ちなみは‥‥）
English: (Is she saying that's when Ms. Deauxnim, who was channeling Dahlia...)

Japanese: （その瞬間、背中から《犯人》に 刺されたわけ、か）
English: (...was stabbed in the back by the killer?)

Japanese: そのとき、あたしは コシが抜けちゃっていて‥‥
English: At that moment, I was paralyzed with fear...

Japanese: 雪の上に、座りこんでいたんです。
English: I was just sitting there in the snow.

Japanese: 血は‥‥きっと地面にも、 パッと飛び散ったと思います。
English: I'm sure blood got on everything around me.

Japanese: じゃあ、そのあとのことは‥‥？
English: So what happened next?

Japanese: ‥‥おぼえてません。
English: ...I don't remember.

Japanese: どうやって助かったのか‥‥ そいつも、おぼえてないのかい？
English: How were you saved? You don't even remember that?
No...

Japanese: めめ、めっそうもないッ！
English: I haven't got any idea!

Japanese: あの‥‥そんなの、 もう‥‥、見当もつきませんっ！
English: Umm... I really don't know what happened at all!

Japanese: ‥‥そいつは、 ザンネンだったな。
English: That's too bad.

Japanese: その《男の人》‥‥ いったい、だれだったの？
English: A man? Who was this man?

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥その。
English: W-Well... Y-You see...

Japanese: さすがに、見えなかったな。
English: I-I couldn't see.

Japanese: 見えなかった‥‥？ （また、か‥‥）
English: You couldn't see...? (She's used this excuse twice now...)

Japanese: だって、灯ろうのあかりなんて、 そんなに明るいわけじゃないから。
English: Well, you know... Lantern light isn't very bright.

Japanese: 《灯ろう》‥‥ですか。
English: Lantern light, did you say?

Japanese: 奥の院の中庭には、 大きな灯ろうがあるんです！
English: There's a great big stone lantern in the garden!

Japanese: 修験者の修行があるときは、 小さな灯をともすんですよ。
English: They always light it when an acolyte is there for training.

Japanese: ほ、ホラ！　よく言うでしょ。 『灯ろう、もと暗し』って！
English: H-Hey! You know what they say... "Under the lantern, darkness reigns."

Japanese: 近くのワルモノは見えずに、 遠くのヒトは見えたわけです！
English: So I could see the person that was further away, but not my attacker, who was closer!

Japanese: あのとき、中庭には、他に 明かりはなかったから‥‥
English: Plus, there weren't any other light sources in the garden at the time...

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: ‥‥“襲撃者”の背後に、 《男の人》が立っていたんだね？
English: ...So there was a man standing behind your attacker?

Japanese: う‥‥うん。
English: Umm... yeah.

Japanese: その《男》が‥‥犯人だ。 背後から、被害者を刺した。
English: That man... He's the killer. He stabbed her from behind.

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみを霊媒した、 天流斎　エリス先生‥‥
English: He's the one who killed Elise Deauxnim...

Japanese: つまり‥‥きみの、おかあさんを。
English: Otherwise known as... Misty Fey, your mother.

Japanese: ‥‥は、はんにん‥‥！
English: The killer...!

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん。
English: Maya...

Japanese: きみは、その犯人の正体を‥‥ 知っているんじゃないかな？
English: You know who killed your mother, don't you.
Umm...

Japanese: ど、どういうことですか、弁護人！
English: Wh-What is the meaning of this, Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: ‥‥この証人は、かなり コンランしているようです。
English: To be frank, Your Honor, I think she is in shock and quite confused.

Japanese: だから‥‥大きなカンちがいを していることに、気づいていない。
English: That's why she hasn't noticed the huge problem with her testimony.

Japanese: な、なんのコト？ “カンちがい”って‥‥
English: H-Huh? What do you mean? What problem...?

Japanese: ‥‥真宵ちゃん。
English: ...Maya.

Japanese: 事件当夜‥‥、あの灯ろうは こわれていたんだよ。
English: On the night of the crime... that stone lantern was out of commission.

Japanese: え‥‥ええっ！
English: Huh... What!?

Japanese: ‥‥そうなんだ。
English: ...It's true.

Japanese: 奥の院の中庭に、 明かりなんて、なかったんだよ！
English: There was no light anywhere in the garden that night.

Japanese: そ‥‥そんなああッ！
English: N-No!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！ い、いかがですか、ゴドー検事！
English: Order! Order in the court! M-Mr. Godot! Explain this!

Japanese: ‥‥コーヒーの澄みきった闇に、 ミルクをそそいで、かきまわす。
English: ...Add the pureness of milk to the perfect, clear darkness of coffee. Stir.

Japanese: 証人のココロは、今‥‥ ニゴりきったカフェ・オ・レだぜ。
English: That is the state of the witness's mind right now -- a cup of café au lait.

Japanese: か、かふぇおれ‥‥ コーヒー牛乳、ですかな？
English: C-Café o' Lay? Is that even legal...?

Japanese: 弁護士のコトバがミルクならば‥‥ アンタはスプーンなのさ、裁判長！
English: Mr. Trite's words are the milk and you are the spoon, Your Honor.

Japanese: こ‥‥この私が、スプーン！
English: I-I'm a spoon!? I'm no spoony bard, I'll have you know!

Japanese: アンタもわかっているハズだ、 まるほどう。
English: You must have noticed it too, Trite.

Japanese: ‥‥この証人の精神状態は、 かなり不安定な状態にある。
English: ...This witness's mental state is highly unstable right now.

Japanese: 多少のカンちがいは、 やむを得ねえだろうぜ！
English: It's not hard to understand why she would make a little mistake like that.

Japanese: ザンネンだけど‥‥ そうは行かない！
English: Sorry, but that's not going to cut it!

Japanese: ‥‥なんだと‥‥？
English: ...What did you say!?

Japanese: 《中庭に、明かりがなかった》 ‥‥それがジジツとなると‥‥
English: If there truly was no light in the garden...

Japanese: さっきの証言には、致命的な ムジュンが生まれるのです！
English: ...then there's a fatal contradiction in the witness's last bit of testimony!

Japanese: な‥‥なるほどくん‥‥
English: N-Nick...

Japanese: ‥‥いいですか。
English: ...May I?

Japanese: 証人は、“襲撃者”について こう証言しています。
English: Recall the witness's statement about her attacker.

Japanese: 『性別も、オトナかコドモかすら わからなかった』‥‥と。
English: She said that she didn't know if it was a man or a woman, an adult or a child.

Japanese: ‥‥それなのに！
English: ...And yet!

Japanese: その襲撃者の後ろに立っていた ハズの人物について、証人は‥‥
English: The witness could describe a person that was standing behind her attacker...

Japanese: ハッキリ《男の人》だったと 証言している！
English: And she quite clearly described him as a man!

Japanese: あ‥‥ッ！
‥‥つまり！
English: ...In other words!

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんには、その人物が “見えていた”ことになる！
English: That would have to mean that Maya actually saw our mystery person!

Japanese: 目の前に立った人物すら見えない はずの、暗闇の中でッ！
English: Despite it being so dark that she couldn't see the face of the attacker in front of her!

Japanese: い‥‥‥‥ いやあああああああああああっ！ N...
English: NOOOOOOOOOO!

Japanese: せ‥‥静粛に！　静粛に！ どういうことですか！　弁護人！
English: Or-Order! Order! Order! What in the world does this all mean, Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: 《暗闇の中で犯人の姿を見た》 などと‥‥！
English: Are you saying Ms. Fey saw the real killer under pitch black conditions!?

Japanese: アンタ‥‥自分の言ってることが わかってるのかい。
English: Trite! Do you have any idea what you're proposing?

Japanese: 闇の中で、どうやって “見る”ことができるんだ！
English: How could she have seen in the dark?

Japanese: 現場には、他の明かりは いっさい、なかったんだぜ！
English: There was no other light source at the scene!

Japanese: 闇の中だからこそ‥‥“見える” ことがあるかもしれませんよ。
English: There are some things that you can only see in the dark, Mr. Godot.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん。きみは、闇の中で 《犯人》の姿を見た。
English: Maya, you did see who the killer was in the dark.

Japanese: そして‥‥、その男を かばおうとしている。
English: And now... you're trying to cover for him.

Japanese: か‥‥“かばう”‥‥ですって！ 自分の母親を殺害した犯人を‥‥
English: C-Cover...!? For the man that killed her mother...!?

Japanese: ‥‥そこから考えられる 結論は、ただひとつ。
English: There's only one conclusion I can draw from this...

Japanese: ‥‥きみは、その《男》の 正体を、知っているんだよ。
English: You know who this man is!

Japanese: おねがい‥‥なるほどくん‥‥
English: Please... Nick...

Japanese: あたし、何も知らないよ。 だから‥‥、もう‥‥‥
English: I don't know anything... Please... I'm begging you...

Japanese: ‥‥クッ！
おもしれえぜ‥‥まるほどう。 聞かせてもらおうじゃねえか。
English: You talk a good game, Trite. But let's see if you can walk the walk.

Japanese: 証人が‥‥闇の中で、 いったい、何を見たのか‥‥？
English: It was pitch black. So what could the witness see?

Japanese: ハッキリさせようぜ！
English: I'm calling your bluff!

Japanese: だ‥‥ダメ！　なるほどくん‥‥ もう、やめて‥‥
English: N-No, Nick! Don't...! P-Please! Stop...!

Japanese: （‥‥真宵ちゃんは、犯人を かばおうと必死だ‥‥）
English: (Maya is dead set on protecting this guy...)

Japanese: （自分の母親を殺害した、 その犯人を‥‥！）
English: (The man who murdered Maya's long-lost mother...!)

Japanese: （でも‥‥、それを 見逃すわけにはいかない）
English: (But... I can't let him get away with it.)

Japanese: （ぼくは《真実》を立証するため、 ここに立っているのだから！）
English: (I'm a lawyer. An officer of the court. I'm here to find the truth!)

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、弁護人に 提示していただきましょう。
English: ...Alright, Mr. Wright. Time to show us what you've got.

Japanese: 証人が闇の中で見た、 《犯人》の正体を‥‥！
English: Who was this person that you say Ms. Fey saw in the darkness...?

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら‥‥ これで、立証されたようです。
English: It looks like you've proven a well-known fact.

Japanese: やはり、闇の中では 犯人など見えない、ということが！
English: The fact that people really CAN'T see in the dark!
Urk...

Japanese: ‥‥アンタ。オレの 思ったとおりだったようだぜ。
English: You're just the man I thought you were...

Japanese: 一生、アマいカフェ・オ・レを 飲むがいいさッ！
English: You should try a nice, sweet café au lait at least once in your life, Trite!

Japanese: （闇の中で《犯人》を“見た”‥‥ これは、ジジツだ！）
English: (B-But I'm sure she saw the killer in the dark...!)

Japanese: （‥‥闇に浮かび上がるもの‥‥ 考えるまでもないだろう！）
English: (So the answer is clear! What is visible in the dark!?)

Japanese: ‥‥闇の中だからこそ、 目撃された《真犯人》‥‥
English: Because it was pitch black, Ms. Fey was able to recognize the killer easily.

Japanese: みなさんも、見てみたいと 思いませんか？
English: I'm sure the court would like to see for itself how this is possible, yes?

Japanese: な‥‥なんですと！ いったい、どうやって！
English: What!? B-But how do you propose to show us something like that!?

Japanese: ‥‥カンタンなことです。
English: ...It's easy.

Japanese: この法廷に、 “闇”を作ればいい。
English: We just need to recreate the conditions of that night.
C-Conditions...?

Japanese: 弁護側は、要請します！
English: Your Honor! The defense officially requests...

Japanese: ‥‥この法廷の明かりを すべて、消してくださいッ！
English: ...that all the lights in this courtroom be turned off!

Japanese: ‥‥こ‥‥これは‥‥ ‥‥まさか‥‥ッ！
English: Wh... Wh... This is... ...But it can't be...!

Japanese: やってくれるじゃねえか‥‥ まるほどう。
English: That was a nice bit of deduction... Trite.

Japanese: ‥‥いかがですか、みなさん‥‥
English: Well, everyone...?

Japanese: これが、闇の中で目撃された 《男》の正体なのです！
English: This is the man Maya saw on the night of the murder!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order! Order!

Japanese: ご‥‥ゴドー検事！ いったい‥‥どういうことですか！
English: Prosecutor Godot! What is the meaning of this!?

Japanese: 弁護人の主張は、あまりに キテレツというほか、ありません！
English: Surely you must be shocked to hear yourself accused of such a thing!

Japanese: なぜ、反論しないのですかッ！
English: Why aren't you denying it!?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　裁判長。 尋ねる相手が、ちがうようだぜ。
English: Ha...! Your Honor. You're asking the wrong person.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですと？
English: Wh-What do you mean by that!?

Japanese: 目撃したことは、本人に聞く‥‥ そいつが、オレのルールだぜ。
English: If you've got a question, ask the witness... That's one of my rules.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん！ ‥‥どうなんだ？
English: Well, Maya!? How about it?

Japanese: きみが現場で見たのは‥‥
English: What you saw that night...

Japanese: あの、赤い３本の光、 だったんじゃないのかな？
English: Was it 3 glowing red lights?

Japanese: いかがですかな‥‥証人？
English: Well, witness? Answer the question.

Japanese: ち‥‥‥ ちがいます！ Y...
English: You're wrong!

Japanese: あたし‥‥そんなもの、 み。見てませんっ！
English: I-I... I never saw that!

Japanese: ま、真宵ちゃん‥‥！
English: M-Maya!

Japanese: ひ、被害者を刺したヒトが 《男の人》だって思ったのは‥‥
English: I thought the person that stabbed my mother was a man...

Japanese: 他に、ちゃんとした 理由があるんです！
English: ...for a totally different reason!

Japanese: な‥‥‥‥‥なんだってェェッ！
English: Wha-Whaat?

Japanese: そ、それはいったい、 どういう‥‥
English: W-Witness... M-Mr. Wright, what the...!?

Japanese: おしゃべりは そこまでだぜ‥‥裁判長。
English: Stop your chattering, Your Honor.

Japanese: お‥‥、おしゃべり‥‥
English: Ch-Ch-Chattering...?

Japanese: ダイジなコトは、証言で聞く。 そいつが‥‥オレのルールだぜ。
English: If it's worth asking, ask the witness. That's one of my rules.

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Alright.

Japanese: それでは、証言を つづけていただきましょう。
English: Well then, let's continue with the testimony.

Japanese: 犯人を《男》であると 知った、その理由を！
English: Please tell us how you knew the killer was a man!

Japanese: ‥‥は、はい。 あれは、事件が起こって‥‥
English: ...Y-Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: あたしが気を失った あとのコトなんですけど‥‥
English: I didn't realize it until after I woke up, but...

Japanese: ～《犯人》について～
English: -- The Killer --

Japanese: ‥‥気がついたら、あたし‥‥ 奥の院に、寝かされていました。
English: ...When I came to, I was just lying there on the Training Hall floor.

Japanese: 中庭に戻ってみると‥‥なぜか 現場はすっかり、変わっていた‥‥
English: By the time I got back to the garden, the place had totally changed...

Japanese: かがり火が燃えていて‥‥ 死体が、消えていました。
English: The torches were lit... And the body was gone.

Japanese: 灯ろうのまわりの雪も、 キレイになくなっていたんです。
English: And all of the snow around the stone lantern had been carefully cleaned up, too.

Japanese: それを、ひとりでやったんだから ‥‥《男の人》だって思いました。
English: Since the person did all that work alone, I just assumed it was a man...

Japanese: ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 《犯人》が男だと思ったのは、 事件の“後”だったわけですか。
English: So it was after the crime took place that the witness came to think the killer was a man.

Japanese: そう‥‥でした。 すみません。あたし‥‥
English: Yes... That's right... I'm sorry, Your Honor, I...

Japanese: ゴドー検事の言うとおり、 あなたは今、つかれています。
English: No need to apologize. It's as Mr. Godot said... You're utterly exhausted.

Japanese: 多少のコンランは やむを得ないでしょう。
English: It's only natural that you would be a little confused.

Japanese: それに‥‥たしかに、 現場の状況を考えれば、
English: Also, if you consider the situation you described...

Japanese: 犯人は男性と思うのも ムリはないようです。
English: ...it doesn't seem too much of a stretch to assume the criminal was a man.

Japanese: それでは、弁護人。 ‥‥尋問をおねがいします！
English: Mr. Wright. Proceed with your cross-examination.

Japanese: ～《犯人》について～
English: -- The Killer --

Japanese: ‥‥気がついたら、あたし‥‥ 奥の院に、寝かされていました。
English: ...When I came to, I was just lying there on the Training Hall floor.

Japanese: 中庭に戻ってみると‥‥なぜか 現場はすっかり、変わっていた‥‥
English: By the time I got back to the garden, the place had totally changed...

Japanese: かがり火が燃えていて‥‥ 死体が、消えていました。
English: The torches were lit... And the body was gone.

Japanese: 灯ろうのまわりの雪も、 キレイになくなっていたんです。
English: And all of the snow around the stone lantern had been carefully cleaned up, too.

Japanese: それを、ひとりでやったんだから ‥‥《男の人》だって思いました。
English: Since the person did all that work alone, I just assumed it was a man...

Japanese: （‥‥たしかに、犯人はかなり、 大がかりなことをしている）
English: (The killer certainly went through a lot of trouble...)

Japanese: （死体を《振り子》でわたし、 現場にも、手を加えた‥‥）
English: (Moving the body by pendulum, manipulating the crime scene...)

Japanese: （いったい、なんのために、 そんなことを‥‥？）
English: (But for what purpose...? Why do all that?)

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんは、気がついて いるのかもしれないな‥‥）
English: (Maybe Maya knows more than she's letting on...)

Japanese: あたしのために‥‥証拠をゼンブ、 かたづけてくれたんだと思います。
English: I think it was for my sake that the killer cleaned up the evidence of what had happened.

Japanese: 気を失ったのは、奥の院の 《中庭》‥‥だったよね？
English: So you lost consciousness when you were in the garden. Is that right?

Japanese: うん。あの‥‥ひ、被害者が 刺された瞬間、だと思う。
English: Yes. I think... I think it happened when the v-victim was stabbed.

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥真宵ちゃんを 奥の院の中へ運んだのは‥‥？
English: So then the person that carried you into the Inner Temple...?

Japanese: 被害者を背中から刺した《犯人》 ということになります‥‥！
English: It could only have been the killer...!

Japanese: あたしも、そう思います。
English: That's what I think, too.

Japanese: （犯人が‥‥真宵ちゃんを、 奥の院の中へ運んだ‥‥）
English: (So the killer carried Maya into the Inner Temple...)

Japanese: もちろん‥‥《犯人》が やったことだろうね。
English: Naturally the killer must have done it, right?

Japanese: ‥‥うん。そうだと思う。
English: Yes, I think so...

Japanese: どうして‥‥犯人は、 現場に細工をしたんだろう？
English: But why would the killer tamper with the crime scene like that?

Japanese: 犯人自身にとって、何か 致命的な証拠でもあったのかな。
English: There must have been something that the killer desperately wanted to hide.

Japanese: あたし‥‥‥ I...
わかりません。
English: I don't know.

Japanese: （‥‥今はまだ、 自信がないみたいだ）
English: (It looks like she doesn't have enough confidence.)

Japanese: （もう少し、情報を集めてから 聞いてみるか‥‥）
English: (I think I need to gather some more information before I ask her again...)

Japanese: もちろん‥‥《犯人》が やったことだろうね。
English: Naturally the killer must have done it, right?

Japanese: ‥‥うん。そうだと思う。
English: Yes, I think so...

Japanese: どうして‥‥犯人は、 現場に細工をしたんだろう？
English: But why would the killer tamper with the crime scene like that?

Japanese: 犯人自身にとって、何か 致命的な証拠でもあったのかな。
English: There must have been something that the killer desperately wanted to hide.

Japanese: あたし‥‥‥ I...
じつは‥‥現場を見て、 感じたことがあるんです。
English: The truth is... When I saw the crime scene, I felt something.

Japanese: “感じたこと”‥‥？
English: You did?

Japanese: ハンニンは‥‥あたしのために、 証拠をかたづけてくれた‥‥って。
English: Yes... I felt like the killer was hiding the evidence for me... for my sake...

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！ “あなたのために”‥‥？
English: Wh-What? Hiding it for you...!?

Japanese: あの晩、奥の院にいたことが わかってるのは、あたしだけです。
English: Everyone knew that I was the only one at the Inner Temple that night.

Japanese: 毘忌尼さんが戻ってきて、 現場を見てしまったら‥‥
English: If Sister Bikini had come back and looked at the garden...

Japanese: ‥‥真宵ちゃんが犯人だと 思ったかもしれない‥‥
English: ...She may have thought that you had done it...

Japanese: ‥‥いや。きっと、 そう思ったでしょうな。
English: ...No, she definitely would have thought so.

Japanese: だから、犯人は‥‥
English: And you're saying that's why the killer...

Japanese: 事件など、なかったかのように 現場をかたづけた、と‥‥？
English: ...cleaned up the crime scene to make it look like nothing had happened...?

Japanese: ‥‥ええ。きっと‥‥ そうだと思うんです。
English: Yes. I'm sure of it.

Japanese: 今の発言は、重要な手がかりに なると考えられます！
English: Well, that's certainly an important piece of information!

Japanese: 証言に加えていただきましょう！
English: I want you to add that to your testimony!

Japanese: ‥‥はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor...

Japanese: かがり火が‥‥燃えていた？
English: The torches were lit?

Japanese: うん。それでわかったんだ。 現場が変わっていたコト。
English: Yes. That's how I noticed that the whole scene had changed.

Japanese: （犯人が火をともした‥‥ そう考えるべきだろうな）
English: (I'm going to say it was the killer who lit the torches... I mean, who else could it be?)

Japanese: 暗闇の中では、死体を運ぶなどの 作業は不可能ですからな。
English: The killer probably lit them since it'd be impossible to do any cover-up work in the dark.

Japanese: しかし‥‥そうなると、 やはり気になりますね。
English: However, if that's true, there's one thing that still bothers me.

Japanese: そもそも、犯人はなぜ 死体を運び出したのでしょうか？
English: Why did the killer go to the effort of moving the body?

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥犯人自身には なんのメリットもない）
English: (That's true... It's hard to see how that would be of any advantage to the killer.)

Japanese: （死体が消えることで メリットがあったのは‥‥）
English: (The only one who would gain anything from that would be...)

Japanese: （奥の院にひとりきりでいた、 真宵ちゃん、かな‥‥）
English: (...the only person that was at the Inner Temple, Maya.)

Japanese: それでは‥‥現場の状況を もう少し聞かせてもらえますかな。
English: Very well... Let me hear some more about the condition of the crime scene.

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥あの雪は、犯人が‥‥？ （ずっと、妙だと思っていた）
English: So you're saying the killer cleaned up the snow? (It did look really odd...)

Japanese: （不自然に切り取られた、 灯ろうのまわりの雪‥‥）
English: (The snow was removed in an unnatural-looking rectangular shape around the lantern...)

Japanese: 奥の院の物置に、 スコップがあったの。
English: There were a lot of shovels around the Inner Temple.

Japanese: とっても、重くて‥‥ あたしには、ムリだったと思う。
English: But they're all really heavy. Way too heavy for me to use.

Japanese: ‥‥なんとも、 ナゾの多い犯人ですなあ。
English: ...An odd fellow indeed, this killer.

Japanese: いったい、なぜ。 雪を持ち去ったのでしょう？
English: Why on earth would anyone want to take snow away?

Japanese: ‥‥たしか‥‥
English: Well there's one thing I can think of...

Japanese: 雪の上には、被害者の血が 飛び散ったんだよね？
English: Didn't you say that a lot of the victim's blood sprayed onto the snow?

Japanese: う、うん‥‥
English: Y-Yeah...

Japanese: 地面に座りこんだあたしにも、 ふりかかったから。
English: The area I collapsed in ended up being splattered.

Japanese: つまり‥‥
English: In other words...

Japanese: 犯人は、血の付着した 雪を、かくすつもりだった。
English: ...the killer's purpose was to hide the bloody snow.

Japanese: そう考えるのが 自然だと思いますけど‥‥
English: I think that's the most reasonable explanation.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm, perhaps...

Japanese: しかし、そう考えたとしても やはりギモンが残ります。
English: However, there's something that's bothering me.

Japanese: 血の付着した雪をかくす だけだったのなら‥‥
English: If the killer just wanted to hide the snow with blood on it...

Japanese: 何も、こんなにたくさん、 雪かきをする必要はありません‥‥
English: ...there was no need to remove that amount.

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥）
English: (That's true...)

Japanese: （血のついたところだけ かたづければ済むハナシだ）
English: (He could have scooped up just the snow that was stained with blood.)

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、この雪には まだ、ナゾがあるようですな。
English: It looks like there are some mysteries behind this issue.

Japanese: ‥‥で、でも。 これで、ハッキリしたと思います！
English: ...But, I think this will help explain them!

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥それまでは、 《犯人》を見ていない、と‥‥？
English: So, you're saying that you never saw the killer, right?

Japanese: う‥‥うん。
English: Y-Yes.

Japanese: でも‥‥さっきは、ハッキリ こう言ってたのに。
English: But earlier you very clearly stated that...

Japanese: 『襲撃者の後ろに、男の人を見た』 ‥‥って。
English: ...you saw a man behind your attacker.

Japanese: ごめんね‥‥なるほどくん。
English: Nick... I'm sorry...

Japanese: ま、真宵ちゃんのために‥‥？
English: They did it for your sake...?

Japanese: だって、死体や血のあとが 残っていたら‥‥
English: Well... If the body and the blood hadn't been removed...

Japanese: 疑われるのは‥‥ あたし、だよね？
English: ...I would've immediately been the prime suspect, right?

Japanese: おそらく‥‥そうでしょうな。
English: Yes, I suppose that's true.

Japanese: （そして、そうなっていたら‥‥ 被害者の《正体》が問題だ）
English: (Yeah, and if that happened, the body would have caused some other problems, too.)

Japanese: （身元がわかったら‥‥ たいへんなことになっていた）
English: (If her real identity had come out right then, it would have been terrible.)

Japanese: （倉院流霊媒道・家元が、 ひとり娘によって、殺害！）
English: ("Master of Kurain Killed by Own Daughter!")

Japanese: （きっと、真宵ちゃんは 耐えられなかっただろうな‥‥）
English: (I don't think Maya would've been able to handle it...)

Japanese: しかし‥‥犯人の行動は ある種、リカイに苦しみます。
English: But still... It's rather difficult to fathom the killer's motives.

Japanese: そこまでして、あなたを守ろうと した《理由》は、なんなのか‥‥？
English: What reason is there to go through that much trouble to protect you...?

Japanese: 被害者・天流斎　エリスの死体は、 葉桜院の境内まで運ばれました。
English: The body of Elise Deauxnim was carried all the way to Hazakura Temple's courtyard.

Japanese: そして、現場の中庭では‥‥
English: Then at the garden, the real scene of the crime...

Japanese: 血が飛び散ったと思われるエリアの 雪が、運び去られている。
English: ...the snow that we suspect was covered in blood was scooped up and removed.

Japanese: これらは、本当の殺人現場を かくすための細工と考えられます。
English: It's reasonable to believe all this was done in an attempt to hide the true crime scene.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし。
English: However...

Japanese: それにしては‥‥ひとつだけ、 どうしても不自然な点があります。
English: There's still one matter that still seems somewhat odd.

Japanese: “不自然”ねえ‥‥。 いったい、なんのことだ？
English: Oh? And what would that be?

Japanese: あなたも、今ならわかるはずです。 ゴドー検事‥‥
English: You must have figured it out by now, Mr. Godot...

Japanese: もちろん、灯ろうに 残された、血文字ですよ。
English: It's the message written in blood on the lantern.

Japanese: 白い灯ろうに、ハッキリ 書かれています。
English: It was written very clearly on the white stone lantern.

Japanese: 《マヨイ》‥‥‥と。
English: "Maya"...
Ah...

Japanese: 犯人の目的が《真宵ちゃんを守る》 ことだったのならば！
English: If the killer was so motivated to protect Maya from suspicion...

Japanese: まっ先に、この灯ろうの文字を 消すはずなのです！
English: ...then why didn't he wipe the writing off the lantern?

Japanese: ああああああッ！ た、たしかに！
English: Aaaaaaah! You're right!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ で‥‥ですが、弁護人！
English: Order! Order! Order! B-But, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: 犯人が、現場をかくそうとしたのは ジジツなのでは‥‥？
English: Isn't it a fact that the killer was trying to cover up the crime scene...?

Japanese: 死体を運びだして、血の付着した 雪をすべてかくしても‥‥
English: Indeed. But it doesn't make much sense to move the body and remove the bloody snow...

Japanese: 最もアヤシイ、この血文字を 残してしまっては、イミがない！
English: ...then not wipe off the most incriminating thing of all -- the bloody writing!

Japanese: それならば‥‥‥
English: But if that's the case...

Japanese: 弁護士さんには‥‥ その《ワケ》が、わかるのかい？
English: ...do you have an explanation for the killer's mysterious behavior?

Japanese: 現場から死体を運びだし、 血痕をかくした犯人が、なぜ‥‥
English: Why would this killer move the body and remove all that snow...

Japanese: その灯ろうの血文字だけは、 現場に残したのか‥‥？
English: ...but then leave the bloody writing on the lantern...?

Japanese: （‥‥犯人の《意図》が どうであろうと‥‥）
English: (I don't know what the killer's plan was...)

Japanese: （灯ろうに文字が残っている ジジツは、動かせない）
English: (...but it's a fact that the killer left the writing on the lantern.)

Japanese: （そこには‥‥かならず 《理由》があるはずだ！）
English: (There must be a reason for it!)

Japanese: それでは成歩堂くん！ あなたの考えをうかがいます。
English: Well then, Mr. Wright! Let's hear your opinion.

Japanese: 犯人は‥‥なぜ、灯ろうの血文字を 消し去らなかったのですか？
English: Why did the killer leave the message written in blood on the lantern?

Japanese: その理由は‥‥ １つしか考えられません。
English: There's only one reason... And it's easier to spot than Sister Bikini in a bikini.

Japanese: 灯ろうの血文字は、 犯人が書いたものなのです！
English: It's because the killer is the one who wrote it!
...Huh?

Japanese: ‥‥やれやれ。
English: ...Too bad, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 先ほどの、タチの悪い証人の 証言を、思い出してください。
English: Don't you remember the testimony of that rather pathetic, vile woman?

Japanese: アタシ‥‥ 刺されたような気がするわ。
English: I think... I think I was stabbed.

Japanese: 意識が、遠くなって‥‥
English: Anyway, I suddenly lost consciousness...

Japanese: アタシ、灯ろうにもたれかかって ‥‥あの、名前を書いたの。
English: But before I did... I scrawled her name on the lantern.

Japanese: せめて‥‥綾里　真宵に 疑いがかかるように‥‥
English: I had hoped it would cast suspicion on Maya.

Japanese: そういえば、そんなコトを 言ってましたっけ。
English: Oh yeah... She did say that, didn't she...?

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ アンタと、美柳　ちなみ‥‥
English: Ha...! You and Dahlia...

Japanese: どっちがタチが悪いか、 競争してみたらどうだい！
English: I'm not sure which one of you is more pathetic!

Japanese: うぐぅぅぅぅッ！
English: Ugggghhh!

Japanese: （これまでの、審理‥‥ そのすべてを、思い出せ！）
English: (I've got to recall everything I've seen and heard up to this point!)

Japanese: （‥‥そこに、かならず コタエがあるはずだ！）
English: (The answer is there somewhere!)

Japanese: その理由は‥‥ １つしか考えられません。
English: There's only one reason! One as obvious as Jean Armstrong in a thong on the Riviera!

Japanese: ‥‥犯人は、真宵ちゃんに 罪を着せようとしたのです！
English: ...The killer was trying to pin the crime on Maya!
...Huh?

Japanese: あのさ。なるほどくん。 あたしが言うのもなんだけど‥‥
English: Umm, Nick. I shouldn't have to be the one to tell you this, but...

Japanese: ハンニンが、あたしに 罪を着せるつもりなら‥‥
English: If the killer had meant to put the blame on me...

Japanese: 現場をそのままにしておけば よかったんじゃないかな。
English: ...then why didn't the killer leave the crime scene as it was?
Oh...

Japanese: いつもどおり、その人差し指は マトはずれ、だったようですな。
English: Once again, you've been pointing that finger where it doesn't belong!

Japanese: ‥‥ゴドー検事。 あなたは、こう言っていましたね。
English: ...Prosecutor Godot. Earlier in this trial, you gave me some good advice.

Japanese: 《あり得ないことを、すべて 消去していけば‥‥》
English: "Once you eliminate the impossible..."

Japanese: 《最後に残るのは‥‥ たった１つの“真実”》
English: "...whatever remains must be the truth."

Japanese: ‥‥イイところは どんどん吸収してくれよ。
English: Maybe you're not as dumb as I thought.

Japanese: この事件の犯人は、本当の現場を かくそうとしていた。
English: The real killer wanted to disguise the fact that a crime occurred there.

Japanese: それならば！
English: If that's the case...

Japanese: 灯ろうの血文字を消さない はずがないのです！
English: They wouldn't have left the bloody writing on the stone lantern on purpose!

Japanese: もし‥‥その血文字に、 気づいていれば‥‥‥
English: Therefore, it must mean that they didn't notice it...

Japanese: で‥‥でも！
English: B-But... That doesn't make any sense!

Japanese: こんなに目立つ血文字を 見落とすなんて‥‥
English: The torches were all lit and everything...

Japanese: ふつう、あり得ないよ！ かがり火も、燃えていたのに‥‥
English: There's no way any normal person would miss something as glaring as that!

Japanese: たしかに‥‥あり得ないね。 “ふつう”なら。
English: You're right... There is no way any "normal" person would.

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうことですか、 弁護人！
English: What...? What are you trying to say, Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: ‥‥この事件の関係者の中に、 たったひとりだけ‥‥
English: There's only one person involved in this incident...

Japanese: 血文字に“気づかなかった” 人物が存在するのです。
English: ...who could have missed seeing the bloody writing altogether.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって‥‥！
English: And who would that be!?

Japanese: 灯ろうの血文字に、気づかなかった 人物‥‥いったい、だれですかッ！
English: Who is the person that could have failed to notice the bloody writing!?

Japanese: ‥‥私も、死ぬときは血文字を 書き残すことにしましょう。
English: ...I think I'll leave some bloody writing when I die, too.

Japanese: 《ナルホド》‥‥と。
English: I'll write... "Wright Left".

Japanese: ど、どうしてですか？
English: H-Huh?

Japanese: どうせ、あなたは 気づかないでしょうからな。
English: You have no idea what you're talking about, do you?

Japanese: （ぼくの目がフシ穴だってコトか）
English: (Is he telling me I need to leave the courtroom...?)

Japanese: ‥‥まるほどう。
English: Hey, Trite...

Japanese: 遊ぶのは、法廷が終わってからに してくれねえか。
English: You can play after the trial is over!

Japanese: （‥‥ぼくの考えに、 マチガイはない！）
English: (I'm sure I'm on the right track!)

Japanese: （これまでの審理を‥‥ もう一度、思い出すんだ！）
English: (I've just got to think more carefully about all that's happened up to this point!)

Japanese: ゴドー検事‥‥
English: Mr. Godot...

Japanese: あなたはきのう、 こう言ってましたね。
English: This is what you said yesterday...

Japanese: ‥‥オレの目は、とっくに イカれちまっているのさ。
English: My eyesight is pretty messed up.

Japanese: こんなデカいメガネをブラ下げて いても‥‥完全には見えねえ。
English: Even with these huge goggles on my head, I still can't see everything.

Japanese: 完全には‥‥見えない！
English: You can't see everything!?

Japanese: そうなのですか、 ゴドー検事‥‥！
English: Is that correct, Mr. Godot!?

Japanese: この灯ろうは、今日の審理で 証拠品として提出されました。
English: This lantern was submitted as evidence today.

Japanese: そのときのことを 思い出してください。
English: I would like the court to think back to the moment it was first presented.

Japanese: ‥‥こ、この灯ろう‥‥ 何か、書いてありますッ！
English: Th-This lantern... There's something written on it!

Japanese: これは‥‥ ち、血文字、ですかッ！
English: Wh-Why... It's written in blood!

Japanese: ば‥‥バカなッ！
English: Hmph... Nonsense!

Japanese: この灯ろうに‥‥ 何か書いてある、ってのか！
English: This lantern... It's as clean as a whistle!

Japanese: ‥‥ゴドー検事。 あなたには‥‥
English: ...Mr. Godot. Just admit it.

Japanese: 見えない色があるのです。 ‥‥いかがですか？
English: There are certain colors you can't see... Correct?

Japanese: 白い地にまぎれた、《赤》。 ‥‥それが、あなたには見えない。
English: You can't see red on a white background, can you?

Japanese: ‥‥そう。 前にも一度、同じことがあった。
English: ...That's right. We went through this once before.

Japanese: 《吐麗美庵》で起こった、 あの毒殺事件の審理‥‥
English: During the poisoning case at Trés Bien...

Japanese: コイツは、オレたちのマドンナが つけていた、エプロンだ。
English: This is the apron the delightful Ms. Byrde was wearing at the time.

Japanese: どうやら‥‥、コーヒーを ひっくり返したらしいな。
English: And somehow spilled coffee on.

Japanese: ところで‥‥さっきから フシギだったのですが‥‥
English: There's something still bothering me, Mr. Godot.

Japanese: なぜ‥‥この血痕について、 セツメイがないのですかな？
English: Why have you not explained the bloodstain to the court?

Japanese: 《血痕》だと‥‥？ なんのコトだ、いったい。
English: Bloodstain? What bloodstain would that be?

Japanese: いやいや！　まっ赤な血が、 このようにドバッとベッタリ‥‥
English: Don't play games, prosecutor! The blood-colored stain that's smeared all over the apron!

Japanese: ば‥‥バカな！ 聞いてねえぞ、オレは！
English: That's ridiculous! No one told me anything about a bloodstain!

Japanese: ‥‥白いエプロンに付着した コーヒーは見えた。しかし‥‥
English: You could see the coffee on the white apron...

Japanese: ケチャップは、見えなかった。 《赤》かったから‥‥
English: ...But you couldn't see the ketchup. Because it was red.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ フシギなものだぜ‥‥
English: Ha...! It's strange...

Japanese: モノクロの写真にしてしまえば、 黒く浮き上がる、この文字が‥‥
English: In a black and white photo, those letters would have appeared black to me...

Japanese: どうして‥‥このオレにだけ、 見えねえんだろうな‥‥？
English: I wonder... Why am I the only one that can't see them...?

Japanese: そ、それでは‥‥ゴドー検事！ あなたは‥‥認めるのですか！
English: S-So then... Mr. Godot! Are you admitting it!?

Japanese: この灯ろうの赤い文字‥‥ あなたには、見えなかったと！
English: Are you admitting that you couldn't see the red writing on the lantern!?

Japanese: ‥‥なあ、ジイさんよォ。 アンタ、知らなかったのかい？
English: ...Hey, gramps. Didn't you know?

Japanese: このオレが、“赤い”紅茶を 決して飲まない、そのワケを！
English: That's the reason why I don't drink red tea!

Japanese: （‥‥今まで、どうしても 確信が持てなかった‥‥）
English: (I wasn't sure about it until now, but...)

Japanese: （あの男が‥‥ ゴドー検事が《犯人》なんて！）
English: (I just can't believe it... Prosecutor Godot is the murderer!)

Japanese: （しかし‥‥ もう、もとへは戻れない）
English: (But... There's no going back now.)

Japanese: （ついに、見えてしまった！ 《真実》が‥‥）
English: (I've finally figured out the truth...!)

Japanese: ‥‥ゴドー検事。 弁護側は、あなたを告発します。
English: ...Mr. Godot. The defense at this time formally accuses you.

Japanese: 天流斎　エリス‥‥綾里　舞子 殺害事件の、真犯人として！
English: You are the murderer of Ms. Elise Deauxnim, also known as Ms. Misty Fey!

Japanese: 信じがたいことですが‥‥
English: It's hard to believe this may be true. However...

Japanese: あなたに不利なジジツが、 ふたたび判明したようです！
English: ...once again Mr. Wright has brought up a disquieting fact about you!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
‥‥有罪を逃れるために 紅茶を飲もうとは思わねえ。
English: Just make sure you don't fill out the indictment in red ink, gramps.

Japanese: だが‥‥。たかがラクガキで 犯人にされるワケにもいかねえ。
English: Come on... How does a little graffiti make me into the killer?

Japanese: しかも‥‥書かれている名前は ヒトちがいときてやがる。
English: Besides, it's not like it's my name that's written there.

Japanese: 犯人に《赤》が見えなかったのは、 まちがいありません！
English: I'm certain that the killer wasn't able to see the color red!

Japanese: ‥‥そいつは、おもしろい。 聞かせてもらおうか。
English: This is rich. Do go on, Trite.

Japanese: あの現場に‥‥ 大きな手がかりが眠っています。
English: The answer is right there at the crime scene.

Japanese: “雪”という手がかりが‥‥。
English: In the snow...

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことですかな？
English: The snow? How so?

Japanese: なぜ‥‥あれだけたくさんの 雪が運び出されたのか‥‥？
English: Well, for example, why did the killer move all that snow...?

Japanese: 裁判長。あなたも おっしゃっていたはずです。
English: Your Honor, you said it yourself.

Japanese: 《血痕を消したかったのなら、 その部分だけ、捨てればいい》‥‥
English: If they wanted to hide the bloody snow, why not take out just that area?

Japanese: “その部分だけ”‥‥ あ！　も、もしかして‥‥
English: Yes, why didn't they take just that area...? Ah! C-Could it be...!?

Japanese: ‥‥犯人には、雪に散った 《赤》が見えなかったのです。
English: Yes. The killer couldn't see the red blood that had seeped into the snow.

Japanese: だから‥‥すべての雪を 捨てなければならなかった！
English: And so, he had to remove all of the snow!

Japanese: 血が飛んだ可能性のある エリアの雪を‥‥すべて！
English: He couldn't be sure of where the blood had landed, so he removed the whole area!

Japanese: “暗くて、血痕が見えなかった” という可能性は、どうだい？
English: Isn't it more likely that the killer couldn't see the blood because it was dark?

Japanese: ‥‥あり得ません。
English: ...Not a chance.

Japanese: 現場には、かがり火が燃えていた。 “見えない”はずがないのです。
English: The torches were all lit. They would have been able to see fine.

Japanese: どうやら‥‥
English: It seems...

Japanese: 審理はここへ来て、新たな 局面をむかえたようです！
English: ...that once again, this trial has taken an unexpected turn, to say the least.

Japanese: 説明をおねがいできますかな！ ‥‥ゴドー検事ッ！
English: Can you explain this, Mr. Godot!?

Japanese: ちょ‥‥ちょっと、 待ってください！
English: W-Wait! Just wait a minute!

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん！
English: Maya!

Japanese: な‥‥なんですかな？　証人。
English: Wh-What is it, witness?

Japanese: ご‥‥ゴドー検事は‥‥ 犯人じゃありません！
English: M-Mr. Godot... isn't the killer...!

Japanese: だって‥‥だって、検事さんが 《奥の院》に来たのは‥‥
English: After all, he didn't even come to the Inner Temple...

Japanese: 事件が起こってから、 ２日もあと‥‥
English: ...until 2 days after the murder took place!

Japanese: ‥‥あの吊り橋が 修理されてからなんです！
English: He didn't show up until after that old bridge got fixed up!

Japanese: おじょうちゃん‥‥‥
English: Maya...

Japanese: アンタに、そんなコトが 証言できるわけ、ねえさ。
English: You can't testify to something like that.

Japanese: ど‥‥どうしてですかっ！ あたし、こう見えても意外と
English: Why...? What do you mean!? I may not look it, but I'm--

Japanese: きゃわわああああっ！
English: Eeeeeeeek!!

Japanese: あの事件が起こった後‥‥ アンタは、存在しなかったはずだ。
English: After the murder happened... you didn't even exist.

Japanese: そ‥‥“存在しなかった”‥‥？
English: She didn't...? I'm afraid I don't follow...

Japanese: ヤレヤレ‥‥ジイさん。 さっき、立証されたばかりだぜ。
English: Are you senile, old man? We established this just a little while ago.

Japanese: この証人は‥‥事件の後、 ずっと意識を失っていた。
English: After the murder, this witness was unconscious for a long time.

Japanese: ‥‥《美柳　ちなみ》を 霊媒していたんだからな。
English: Because she was channeling Dahlia Hawthorne.

Japanese: あ‥‥‥ そうでしたな‥‥。
English: Oh... That's right...

Japanese: お‥‥おねがいです！ 証言させてください！
English: P-Please, Your Honor! Let me add to my testimony!

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん！ 聞いてくれるよね‥‥？
English: ...Nick! Please listen to me...!

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん‥‥）
English: (Maya...)

Japanese: （どうあっても、ゴドー検事を かばおうとするつもりか‥‥）
English: (Do you plan to cover for Godot no matter what the cost...?)

Japanese: （それならば‥‥やむを得ない）
English: (If that's the case, then I've got no choice.)

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長！ 証言を聞きましょう。
English: ...Your Honor! Let's hear her testimony.

Japanese: ‥‥それで《真相》に 近づけるのならば。
English: If it means we're going to hear the whole truth, I say we should not silence her.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ いいカクゴだ、まるほどう。
English: Ha...! Nicely done, Trite.

Japanese: それでは、証人に 証言をおねがいします。
English: Very well, let's hear the witness's testimony.

Japanese: 事件があってから‥‥ 《奥の院で何があったか》？
English: Please tell us what happened at the Inner Temple after the murder.

Japanese: あのあと‥‥あたしは霊媒をして、 意識はとぎれちゃいましたけど‥‥
English: Yes, sir. After I woke up, I began channeling and my spirit "left me", as it were.

Japanese: 《奥の院》には、あたしの他に あの子がいたんです！
English: But that little girl was there at the Inner Temple, too!

Japanese: ～事件後の《奥の院》～
English: -- After the Incident --

Japanese: 《奥の院》側には、はみちゃんも 閉じこめられていました！
English: Pearly was also stuck on the Inner Temple side that night!

Japanese: 夜が明けて、人を探したけど‥‥ だれもいなかったそうです。
English: The next morning, she looked around, but couldn't find anyone.

Japanese: 次の日、橋が修理されたとき あの子は《はなれ》にいて‥‥
English: The next day, when the bridge was finally fixed, she was in the spare prep room.

Japanese: ゴドー検事は、そのときに初めて 《奥の院》に来たんです！
English: That's when Mr. Godot arrived at the Inner Temple for the first time!

Japanese: まっ先に、はみちゃんを見つけて 元気づけてくれたんですよ！
English: He found Pearly first, and cheered her right up!

Japanese: “はみちゃん”‥‥？
English: Who is this "Pearly"...?

Japanese: あたしのイトコです！ 綾里　春美。
English: That's my little cousin! Pearl Fey.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥。それで、 今の話は、いつ聞いたのですかな？
English: Hmm... So when did you hear about this?

Japanese: あ。ついさっきです。 ‥‥医務室で。
English: Oh, just awhile ago... When I was in the medical office.

Japanese: まことにザンネンですが‥‥
English: I'm terribly sorry...

Japanese: 他人から聞いたことは、 証言として扱われないのですよ。
English: But what you heard from someone else is simply not admissible as testimony.

Japanese: えええええッ！　そんなあ！
English: Whaaaat!? Come on!

Japanese: はみちゃん、ゼッタイ ウソなんかつきませんよ！
English: Pearly would never tell a lie!

Japanese: あたしなんかより、 よっぽどイイ子なんですから！
English: She's a way more honest person than I'll ever be!

Japanese: （‥‥その真宵ちゃんが 証言してるんじゃないか‥‥）
English: (Real smart, Maya. You always know the best things to say when you're under oath...)

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
‥‥検察側に異議はねえ。 その証人を、信用するぜ。
English: The prosecution has no objection. We believe the witness.

Japanese: ご‥‥ゴドー検事！
English: M-Mr. Godot!

Japanese: 尋問とシャレこもうじゃねえか。 弁護側に異議がなければ‥‥
English: Let's just move on to the cross-examination... If the defense has no objections...

Japanese: い‥‥‥いかがですか、 弁護人！
English: Th-This is highly unusual, but... Well, Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: それでは‥‥ 尋問とシャレこみましょうか。
English: Let's get this cross- examination started.

Japanese: ～事件後の《奥の院》～
English: -- After the Incident --

Japanese: 《奥の院》側には、はみちゃんも 閉じこめられていました！
English: Pearly was also stuck on the Inner Temple side that night!

Japanese: 夜が明けて、人を探したけど‥‥ だれもいなかったそうです。
English: The next morning, she looked around, but couldn't find anyone.

Japanese: 次の日、橋が修理されたとき あの子は《はなれ》にいて‥‥
English: The next day, when the bridge was finally fixed, she was in the spare prep room.

Japanese: ゴドー検事は、そのときに初めて 《奥の院》に来たんです！
English: That's when Mr. Godot arrived at the Inner Temple for the first time!

Japanese: まっ先に、はみちゃんを見つけて 元気づけてくれたんですよ！
English: He found Pearly first, and cheered her right up!

Japanese: （‥‥ここで、重要なのは ただ、１点‥‥）
English: (There's only one thing of any importance here...)

Japanese: （事件が起こったとき‥‥ ゴドー検事が、どこにいたか？）
English: (Where was Godot when the murder was taking place?)

Japanese: （そう。あのとき、彼は すでに《奥の院》にいたハズだ）
English: (He must have already been at the Inner Temple when it happened.)

Japanese: （そうでなければ‥‥ 天流斎　エリスを刺せない！）
English: (Otherwise, he couldn't have killed Elise Deauxnim!)

Japanese: 春美ちゃん‥‥その晩は、 どこで夜を明かしたのかな。
English: So where did do you think Pearls slept that night?

Japanese: 修験堂のワキにある、《はなれ》 にいたんじゃないかな。
English: In the spare prep room next to the Training Hall, I guess.

Japanese: 修行中の修験堂には、入っちゃ いけないキマリがあるから。
English: There's a rule that you can't enter the Training Hall during an acolyte's training.

Japanese: それにしても‥‥その子は なぜ、《奥の院》へ‥‥？
English: But even so, why did she go to the Inner Temple in the first place?

Japanese: なんでも、真宵ちゃんのことが シンパイになったそうです。
English: It seems that Pearls became very worried about Maya.

Japanese: 霊行道の修行、かなり 荒っぽいですから‥‥。
English: She knew that the spiritual training I was about to undergo was very intense.

Japanese: （春美ちゃんは、《美柳　ちなみ》 を霊媒するはずだった）
English: (Pearls was supposed to channel Dahlia Hawthorne...)

Japanese: （でも、できなかった。‥‥だから そのまま《奥の院》へ向かった）
English: (But she couldn't do it. That's why she headed to the Inner Temple.)

Japanese: （しかし‥‥そこには、すでに 《美柳　ちなみ》がいたんだ）
English: (However, Dahlia Hawthorne was already there...)

Japanese: （天流斎　エリスさんの身体に、 その魂を宿らせて‥‥）
English: (...possessing the body of Elise Deauxnim.)

Japanese: 《奥の院》側を、すみずみまで 探したわけだね？
English: She searched the entire Inner Temple side, end to end...?

Japanese: はみちゃん‥‥ そうとう、心細かったみたい。
English: Well, you see... Pearly gets pretty scared when she's alone.

Japanese: 探すところなんて、ほとんど なかったと思うけど‥‥
English: And there weren't that many places to look...

Japanese: 何回か、見て回ったそうです。
English: She says that she even went back and forth several times.

Japanese: 《奥の院》側にあるのは‥‥ 《修験堂》と《はなれ》‥‥
English: The Inner Temple side has two structures: the Training Hall and a spare prep room...

Japanese: ‥‥あと、大きな物置もあるけど。 それぐらいかな。
English: And there's also a storage shack, I think... That's about it.

Japanese: （“ものおき”‥‥か）
English: (There was a storage shack, too?)

Japanese: ‥‥春美ちゃんは、ふつうに 人を探しただけだよね。
English: ...Pearls was looking for other people, right?

Japanese: 物置なんか、 ちゃんと調べたのかな‥‥？
English: Would she have really examined a storage shack all that carefully...?

Japanese: あた、あたしなら まっ先に調べるけどね！
English: Well, if it was me, that would be the first place I would investigate!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: だれかが物置にかくれることは できた、ということですか‥‥
English: So that means it's possible that someone may have been hiding in the storage shack.

Japanese: 春美ちゃん‥‥橋を修理する 音は、聞こえなかったのかな。
English: Pearls didn't hear the sound of them working on the bridge?

Japanese: どういうこと？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: いや。なんで《はなれ》に いたのかな、と思って。
English: I was just wondering why she would stay in the prep room.

Japanese: ぼくなら、橋のたもとに出て 手のひとつも振るかな、って。
English: If it was me, I'd at least go out and wait at the foot of the bridge.

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。はみちゃんね。 そのとき、寝てたんだって。
English: ...Oh. Well, Pearly said she was sleeping at the time.

Japanese: 前の晩、ひとりぽっちで、 心細くて‥‥
English: She said she was so scared during the night...

Japanese: 何度も目がさめちゃった みたいだから。
English: ...that she couldn't sleep well and woke up a bunch of times.

Japanese: いやはや‥‥かわいそうに。 “ひとりぽっち”‥‥とは。
English: Poor little girl... All alone like that...

Japanese: まあ‥‥少なくとも、修験洞には お友だちがいたワケだがな。
English: Well, we know that she had at least one friend in the Sacred Cavern.

Japanese: ‥‥からくり錠と格闘していた 《美柳　ちなみ》が‥‥
English: ...Dahlia Hawthorne, who was busy battling with the trick lock...

Japanese: （彼女は、ひとに姿を見せる ワケには行かなかった‥‥）
English: (She couldn't let anyone see her...)

Japanese: （だから、ふたりはカオを 会わせていないワケだな）
English: (So she wouldn't have shown herself to Pearls.)

Japanese: ‥‥やがて、橋は修理された。 それから、どうなったんだい。
English: What happened after the bridge was finally fixed...?

Japanese: “そのときに、初めて”‥‥ そうなんですか？　ゴドー検事。
English: So it was your first time on the Inner Temple side, Mr. Godot?

Japanese: おっと‥‥。証言台をこえて、 ここまで質問が飛んできやがった。
English: Hmm? That's funny. Am I imagining things, or did the defense ask me a question?

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。質問は、 証人だけにしてもらえますかな。
English: ...Mr. Wright, please save your questions for the witness.

Japanese: 《他人から聞いたことは、 証言として扱われない》‥‥
English: "What you heard from someone else is simply not admissible as testimony."

Japanese: そう言ったのは、あなたです。 ‥‥裁判長。
English: Those are your words. ...Your Honor.

Japanese: これは１本、取られました。
English: Touché, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥しかたありません。 いかがですか？　ゴドー検事。
English: Oh well... What do you have to say, Mr. Godot?

Japanese: アツい夏に、アツいコーヒー‥‥ そいつが、オレのアツい主義さ。
English: Hot nights and even hotter coffee... That's what I always say.

Japanese: 事件がなければ、一生 訪れることはなかったろうぜ。
English: If it hadn't have been for this case, I never would have visited there.

Japanese: ‥‥あんなサムい山奥の、 サムい山寺なんぞに‥‥な。
English: A freezing cold temple in the mountains... I think I'll pass!

Japanese: （《葉桜院》にも《奥の院》にも、 訪れたことがなかった‥‥、か）
English: (So he had never visited Hazakura Temple or the Inner Temple, huh...?)

Japanese: ‥‥クッ！ 何か言いたそうだな、まるほどう。
English: ...Ha! You want to say something, Trite?

Japanese: （とにかく‥‥ゴドー検事の 《スキ》を見つけるしかない！）
English: (In any case, I have to find a crack in Godot's armor!)

Japanese: （‥‥真宵ちゃんの 尋問をしながら‥‥）
English: (While I cross-examine Maya, that is...)

Japanese: （やっぱり、思ったとおりだ）
English: (It's just like I thought.)

Japanese: （ゴドー検事は‥‥ ウソをついている！）
English: (Godot is lying!)

Japanese: （もし、事件発生後に“初めて” 奥の院に行ったのなら‥‥）
English: (If his first visit to the Inner Temple really was after the incident...)

Japanese: （彼は、知っているハズがない！ あの《証拠品》のコトを‥‥）
English: (He shouldn't know about that piece of evidence!)

Japanese: それでは、証言をつづけて いただきましょう。
English: Very well, please go on with your testimony.

Japanese: 橋の修理が終わり、警官たちが 《奥の院》側にわたったのですな。
English: After fixing the bridge, the policemen came over to the Inner Temple side, right?

Japanese: はい！ それで、ゴドー検事は‥‥
English: Yes! Then Mr. Godot...

Japanese: げ、元気づけた‥‥？
English: H-He cheered her up...?

Japanese: はみちゃんが言ってたよ。 『やさしいオジさまでした』って。
English: That's what Pearly said... She said he was a very nice older gentleman.

Japanese: ありがとうございます、 ゴドー検事さん！
English: Thank you for looking after my cousin, Mr. Godot!

Japanese: ただのコーヒー好きではないと 思っていました。
English: And here I was thinking you were nothing more than a coffee-addict.

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　よせやい。 ‥‥テレちまうぜ。
English: Ha...! Cut it out... ...You're making me blush.

Japanese: （なんかクヤシイな‥‥）
English: (This guy is really beginning to get on my nerves... In more ways than one...)

Japanese: 《奥の院》側には、そもそも 見るべきものが少ない。
English: The truth is there aren't that many places to look on the Inner Temple side.

Japanese: 警官たちはみんな、中庭の 手がかりにムチュウだった。
English: The policemen were all busy going over the garden with fine-toothed combs.

Japanese: だから‥‥ひとりで捜査 してたのさ。‥‥“オレ流”に。
English: So I decided to carry out an investigation in my own way... Godot-style!

Japanese: 私も、いつもココロがけています。 ‥‥“オレ流”の判決を。
English: I'm the same way. I like to hand down verdicts in my own way, Judge-style!

Japanese: （ぼくも、なんか聞いてみるか？ “オレ流”に‥‥）
English: (Hmm, maybe I should ask some questions, Phoenix-style...)

Japanese: いかがですか、弁護人。 何かありますかな？
English: How about it, Mr. Wright? Do you have anything to add?

Japanese: いえ。やめておきます。 ‥‥“オレ流”に。
English: No. I give up... This whole Phoenix-style thing, I mean.

Japanese: どうやら‥‥
English: It seems...

Japanese: “オレ流”の使い方が、まだ イタについていないようですな。
English: ...this phrase has become overused, so I'm putting an end to it... Judge-style.

Japanese: （よけいなお世話だ！）
English: (Then let it go already!)

Japanese: 春美ちゃんを、元気づけた‥‥？
English: So you cheered Pearls up?

Japanese: オレが、あの少女を 見つけたとき‥‥
English: When I found that little girl...

Japanese: あの子が、まっ先に聞いたのは 綾里　真宵のことだった。
English: ...the first thing she asked about was her cousin, Maya Fey.
Really...?

Japanese: ‥‥橋が燃え落ち、火のない ボロ小屋に閉じこめられて‥‥
English: The bridge had burned down and she was huddled up in that tiny shack with no heat...

Japanese: ひとり、死ぬほど恐ろしい 一夜をすごすハメになったのに‥‥
English: Even though she must have had a truly terrifying night out there...

Japanese: 自分のことより、まず アンタのコトを聞いたのさ。
English: ...she asked about you before she said a thing about herself.

Japanese: は‥‥はみちゃん‥‥
English: P-Pearly...

Japanese: 《奥の院》側に、アンタが いないことは、気づいていた。
English: I noticed that you weren't anywhere on the Inner Temple side.

Japanese: しかし‥‥それを少女に告げる コトは、オレにはできなかった。
English: But... I couldn't find it in me to tell her that.

Japanese: だからオレは、残っていた最後の １杯を、ごちそうしたのさ。
English: So I gave her my last cup.

Japanese: ミルクとサトウで、現実の苦さを 包みかくして‥‥
English: With milk and sugar... To hide the bitterness of the harsh truth.

Japanese: ‥‥ジツに、ココロあたたまる お話ですな‥‥
English: Wh-What a sweet story... *sniffle*

Japanese: （ゴドー検事‥‥ ポット持参だったのか‥‥）
English: (He had a thermos of coffee? Why doesn't that surprise me?)

Japanese: “捜査”というと‥‥ 何か、手がかりでも？
English: You said that you conducted an investigation of your own. Did you find anything?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥、オレの捜査のウデは まるほどう並、らしいぜ。
English: It looks like my investigation went about as well as yours, Trite.

Japanese: なにぶん‥‥灯ろうの文字すら、 見落としちまうぐらいだからな。
English: After all, I did miss the bloody writing on the lantern.

Japanese: （‥‥ぼくは見落としてないぞ！）
English: (Well, I didn't miss it, so speak for yourself, Goggles!)

Japanese: しいて、変わったことと言えば‥‥ 《修験堂》のビジン、か。
English: The only odd thing I discovered was the beauty in the Training Hall.

Japanese: ビジン‥‥？
English: Beauty...?

Japanese: モチロン‥‥、綾里　舞子さ。
English: ...Misty Fey, naturally.

Japanese: 和服のビジンが、インドの色と 香りに包まれていやがった。
English: Clad in her stunning Japanese garb surrounded by the hue and aroma of Western tastes.

Japanese: （‥‥カレーって インドの色なのか‥‥？）
English: (Western tastes...? Could he find a stranger way to describe gravy...?)

Japanese: それから‥‥《はなれ》へ 向かったワケですかな？
English: So from there you headed for the prep room?

Japanese: （今の、ゴドー検事の証言‥‥）
English: (Wait a sec... What did Godot say just now?)

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥ つかまえたような気がする！）
English: (I think I just found his proverbial weak spot!)

Japanese: ‥‥ゴドー検事。
English: ...Mr. Godot.

Japanese: あなたが橋をわたって、 《奥の院》に行ったのは‥‥
English: The first time you crossed Dusky Bridge and went into the Inner Temple...

Japanese: 事件が起こるよりも、 ずっと《前》だったんですよ。
English: ...was long before the murder took place.

Japanese: ど‥‥どうして そんなことがわかるの！
English: Wh-Why do you say that!?

Japanese: 彼は、たった今‥‥ 致命的なミスを犯したんだ。
English: Because he just made one fatal slip-up.

Japanese: ‥‥《修験堂》にかかっていた、 あの掛け軸について。
English: The hanging scroll in the Training Hall...

Japanese: かけじく‥‥ですか？
English: Hanging scroll...?

Japanese: ‥‥で、でも！
English: B-But!

Japanese: ゴドー検事、まちがってないよ！ あれはたしかに、おかあさんが‥‥
English: Mr. Godot is right! That scroll shows a picture of my mother!

Japanese: ‥‥真宵ちゃん。 きみはモチロン、知らない。
English: Maya... I know you know who it is, but here's something you didn't know.

Japanese: 事件が起こってから、２日後。 おぼろ橋の修理が終わったとき‥‥
English: By the time the bridge had been repaired, two days after the murder...

Japanese: 修験堂にかかっていた掛け軸は‥‥ こうなっていたんだ。
English: ...the hanging scroll in the Training Hall looked like this.

Japanese: なな、なんですか！ この、食欲をそそるニオイは！
English: What's that wonderfully delicious smell!?

Japanese: 事件の翌朝‥‥ある人物が、 カレーをぶちまけたのです。
English: The morning after the crime, someone covered it with gravy.

Japanese: な‥‥なんでまた、 カレーなの？　なるほどくん‥‥
English: Gravy...? But why gravy, Nick?

Japanese: その人物にとっては、 深いワケがあったんだよ。
English: Because gravy was much more than a condiment to the culprit.

Japanese: いかがですか、ゴドー検事！
English: Well, Mr. Godot!?

Japanese: もし、あなたが事件の《後》、 初めて掛け軸を見たのなら‥‥
English: If you really hadn't seen the hanging scroll until after the murder...

Japanese: 《綾里　舞子》が描かれている ことなど、知っているはずがない！
English: ...you wouldn't have had any way of knowing that it was Misty Fey!

Japanese: ちょ‥‥ちょっと待って、 なるほどくん！
English: W-Wait a minute, Nick!

Japanese: ‥‥なんだい。
English: ...Yes?

Japanese: か、掛け軸を もう一度、よく見てよ！
English: T-Take another look at the hanging scroll!

Japanese: ほら！　上のほうに‥‥ 《紋》が描いてあるでしょ！
English: Look! At the top...! There's a crest there!

Japanese: たしか‥‥“家元”を あらわすマーク、だったね。
English: Ah, that. It's the mark of the Master, correct?

Japanese: そうだよ！　だから、この《紋》の イミを知っていれば‥‥
English: Exactly! So if you know the meaning of the mark...

Japanese: 掛け軸に《綾里　舞子》が描かれて いることは、わかりますッ！
English: ...Then you could guess that it was a picture of Misty Fey on there!

Japanese: ‥‥ゴドー検事は、掛け軸について こうも言っています。
English: True, but Mr. Godot described what was underneath like this.

Japanese: 『和服のビジンが、インドの色と 香りに包まれていた』‥‥
English: "Clad in her stunning Japanese garb surrounded by the hue and aroma of Western tastes."
Oh...

Japanese: 《紋》のイミを知ることは 可能だったかもしれない。
English: Yes, it's possible that he knew what the crest meant.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし！
English: ...However!

Japanese: 綾里　舞子の絵姿が和服を着て いたことは、知ることができない！
English: He couldn't have known that she was wearing Japanese clothing!

Japanese: ゴドー検事！ あなたは、あの日‥‥
English: Mr. Godot! On the day of the murder...

Japanese: 事件が起こる《前》から 奥の院にひそんでいたのです！
English: ...you were hiding at the Inner Temple, long before the crime took place!

Japanese: ひとつ‥‥聞いてもいいかい、 まるほどう‥‥
English: Can I ask you just one little thing, Trite?

Japanese: ‥‥なんでしょう。
English: ...What is it?

Japanese: アンタの《言いがかり》‥‥
English: This whole theory of yours...

Japanese: ‥‥ある“前提”がなければ 成立しねえよなァ。
English: It all rests on a certain assumption.

Japanese: 《このオレが‥‥前もって、 犯行が起こるのを知っていた》
English: That I knew beforehand that a crime was going to be committed.

Japanese: そ‥‥そうだよ！
English: Th-That's right!

Japanese: そうでなきゃ、現場にひそんで おくなんて、ムリだもん‥‥
English: Otherwise there's no reason for him to sneak onto the crime scene...

Japanese: モチロン、ゴドー検事は、 この計画を知っていたはずです。
English: Of course Mr. Godot knew about the plan.
Huh...?

Japanese: な‥‥なんですと！
English: Wh-What did you say!?

Japanese: 第三者が、今回の犯行計画を 知っていた可能性があるのですか！
English: Is it really possible that another person knew of that plan!?

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂くん。
English: ...Mr. Wright.

Japanese: この局面で‥‥ 当てずっぽうは通用しません。
English: At this stage of the game... a mere guess is simply unacceptable.

Japanese: ‥‥ここまで来て、たよりは ハッタリだけ、か‥‥
English: ...So in the end, you were just bluffing, huh?

Japanese: 情けねえぜ、まるほどお！
English: Well, don't expect any sympathy cards from me, Trite!

Japanese: （前もって、犯行を知る方法‥‥ １つしか、ないじゃないか！）
English: (But there's only one way he could have known about the crime!)

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。あなたの考えを もう一度だけ、うかがいましょう。
English: ...Mr. Wright, I'll give you just one more chance.

Japanese: ‥‥この犯行が計画されたのは‥‥ 今から、１年以上も前でした。
English: ...This crime was actually planned over a year ago.

Japanese: 綾里　キミ子が、“娘のために” 書いた、この計画書は‥‥
English: Morgan Fey authored the plan for her daughter's future...

Japanese: １年間、綾里家の屋敷のどこかに かくされていたのです。
English: ...and these instructions were hidden somewhere in Fey Manor for a year...

Japanese: そして‥‥綾里　春美が、 その計画書を手にしたとき‥‥
English: However, by the time little Pearls found these instructions...

Japanese: ‥‥それはすでに、 開封されていたそうです。
English: ...they had already been unsealed.

Japanese: か‥‥かいふう‥‥
English: Unsealed...?

Japanese: ‥‥そう。犯人は前もって、 これを読んでいたのです。
English: Yes. The killer had read these instructions long before Pearls ever found them.

Japanese: だから‥‥あの日。《奥の院》での 犯行を、知っていた！
English: That's how he knew the crime was to take place at the Inner Temple!

Japanese: その、ヒキョウな“犯人”が ‥‥このオレだと？
English: And you're saying this crafty killer... is me?

Japanese: ‥‥そうです。
English: You bet I am.

Japanese: だが、アンタ‥‥言ってたな。 計画書は《かくされていた》と。
English: But you just said that the instructions were hidden...

Japanese: そ‥‥そうだよ！
English: Th-That's right!

Japanese: その場所を、ゴドー検事が 知ってるはず、ないじゃない！
English: Mr. Godot couldn't have known where the instructions were hidden!

Japanese: 知ろうと思えば‥‥ たった１つだけ、機会があります。
English: If he really wanted to know, he had one great chance to find out.

Japanese: そ、それはいったい‥‥？
English: Y-Yes, and when was that...?

Japanese: ‥‥面会です。
English: ...During a visit.

Japanese: めんかい‥‥？
English: A visit?

Japanese: 春美ちゃんが、綾里　キミ子から かくし場所を聞いたのは‥‥
English: Morgan Fey told her daughter, Pearl, about where the instructions were hidden...

Japanese: 刑務所の面会室でした。
English: ...during one of her visits to the detention center.

Japanese: ‥‥かくし場所を知るには、 そのときの会話を聞くしかない。
English: That would be the only time for someone to have learned where they were hidden.

Japanese: め‥‥面会中の会話を‥‥ “盗聴”ですって！
English: Ea-Eavesdropping on a visit at the detention center!

Japanese: 刑務所に自由に出入りできる 人物なら、可能でしょう。
English: Yes, it could be arranged if you were someone with easy access in and out of there.

Japanese: たとえば‥‥、そう。 あなたですよ！　ゴドー検事！
English: Like for example, a prosecutor such as Mr. Godot!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order! Order!

Japanese: ご‥‥ゴドー検事‥‥ またしても、あなたですか！
English: M-Mr. Godot...! You're under fire again!

Japanese: ‥‥この犯行は、前もって計画を 知っていなければ、不可能です。
English: ...This murder could not have been carried out without prior knowledge!

Japanese: そして、あなたには‥‥
English: And you...!

Japanese: あなたにだけは、 その方法があった！
English: You were the only one that could've acquired this information before the murder.

Japanese: ‥‥人は、闇を恐れる。
English: ...Humans are afraid of the dark.

Japanese: だが‥‥闇に、オレたちを魅了する フシギな力があるのも、ジジツだ。
English: And yet... At the same time, we're fascinated and bewitched by it.

Japanese: たぶん‥‥そいつが、 人がコーヒーを飲む理由だろうぜ。
English: Maybe that's why humans drink the darkness that is coffee.

Japanese: あの。毎度スミマセンが、 どういうコトですか‥‥？
English: Umm... Sorry for always asking, but what does that mean?

Japanese: ものごとには、かならず 《理由》がある、ってコトさ。
English: It means there's a reason for everything.

Japanese: アンタの主張によれば、 この事件の《犯人》は‥‥
English: According to your theory, the killer in this case eavesdropped...

Japanese: まず、面会室の会話を盗聴して、 犯行の計画書を探しだした。
English: ...on a conversation during a jail visit where he learned of a hidden plan for a crime.

Japanese: そして、その計画を先回りして 《奥の院》に身をかくして‥‥
English: After discovering the plans, he hid in the Inner Temple and waited for the crime to occur.

Japanese: ついには、人の命まで奪った。
English: Then he ultimately took a person's life.

Japanese: ‥‥ただ、この証人を守るために。
English: ...And he did all of that just to protect this witness?

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおりです。
English: That's right.

Japanese: しかし‥‥いったい、なぜだ？
English: If you're accusing me of this crime, I have to ask you... Why would I do this?

Japanese: オレにとって、この少女は‥‥ いわば、赤の他人だ。
English: This girl is nothing but a stranger to me.

Japanese: そこまでして守る、 《理由》がねえのさ。
English: I've got no reason to go through that kind of trouble to protect her.

Japanese: ‥‥オレは見た目ほど、 正義の味方じゃねえんだぜ。
English: I am what you see. I am certainly not the type to rescue the damsel in distress.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 《犯人》の行動は‥‥ ある意味、異常と言えます。
English: The killer's behavior is... certainly... extreme... for lack of a better word.

Japanese: いくら、この証人の命を 守るためとはいえ‥‥
English: Even considering that the killer wanted to protect this witness's life...

Japanese: その行動は、 あまりに不自然です！
English: ...his behavior is still a little too unnatural!

Japanese: おそらく‥‥
English: However...

Japanese: ゴドー検事。 あなたには《理由》があった。
English: ...you had a good reason didn't you, Mr. Godot?

Japanese: この証人、綾里　真宵を守る、 絶対的な理由が‥‥
English: An unshakable reason that forced you to protect this witness at all costs!

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥
English: I knew it...

Japanese: もう、気づいていたんだね。 なるほどくん‥‥
English: You've figured it out, haven't you, Nick...?

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん‥‥）
English: (Maya...)

Japanese: （きみが必死に、ゴドー検事を かばおうとしていたのも‥‥）
English: (I guess you were doing your best to cover for Godot...)

Japanese: （きっと‥‥ 同じ《理由》なんだろう？）
English: (...for the same reason, huh?)

Japanese: 準備はいいぜ、まるほどう。 ‥‥聞かせてもらおうか。
English: OK, Trite. I'm all ears. ...Let's hear it.

Japanese: ‥‥カンタンなことです。
English: It's very simple...

Japanese: あなたにとって‥‥綾里　真宵は “赤の他人”ではなかった。
English: Maya Fey is a lot more than just a stranger to you.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって‥‥！
English: Wh-What's this...!?

Japanese: ある人物の存在が‥‥ すべてを物語っています。
English: There's one person who lies at the very center of this whole story.

Japanese: あなたと、綾里　真宵を 結ぶ‥‥この人物が！
English: One person that ties you and Maya Fey together inextricably!

Japanese: ‥‥ザンネンだが‥‥ 今のオレは、ヒトリぼっちだぜ。
English: ...Sorry, but at this point in my life, I'm a lone wolf.

Japanese: そんなヤツとは、 なんのつながりもねえさ。
English: I've got no connection to that person whatsoever.

Japanese: ちなみに、私も、そんな人物とは つながりはありませんぞ、弁護人。
English: Yes, and by the way, I don't have any connection with them either, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: （何をやってるんだ、ぼくは‥‥）
English: (What am I doing...?)

Japanese: （アイツの“正体”を考えれば‥‥ わかりきったことじゃないか！）
English: (If I think about Godot's true identity, the answer is obvious!)

Japanese: どうだい？　まるほどう。
English: How's it going, Trite?

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥アンタには、 荷が重かったかな‥‥？
English: I knew it... Has this whole trial just been too much for you...?

Japanese: ‥‥もしかしたら、 そうかもしれません。
English: ...Yeah, maybe. I'm not sure...

Japanese: でも、これだけはジジツです！
English: But I am sure about one thing!

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong this trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: Nor is there any need for more time to decide the case against the defendant.

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
English: This case is extremely clear. I see no room for mis- interpretation of the facts.

Japanese: 被告人、葉桜院　あやめに たった今、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Iris of Hazakura Temple...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately...

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: ...to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!
